title: User-Defined Functions
text: User-Defined Functions In Uniface, it is possible to create user-defined functions (also known as private functions) that can be used in the ProcScript language just as any other function. To define your own function, define a function with a return type. On the first line after function , use the returns statement to define the return type. For example: function MULTIPLY returns numeric params numeric PAR1: IN numeric PAR2: IN endparams variables numeric MultiplyResult endvariables MultiplyResult = PAR1 * PAR2 return MultiplyResult end Copy You call a user-defined function just as you would any other function. For example: $1 = MULTIPLY(2, 4) ; $1 equals 8 Copy The visibility of a user-defined function depends on the level where it is defined: A function that is defined in a field-level Script container can be called only from other modules that are defined at the level of that field. A function that is defined in an entity-level Script container can be called only from other modules that are defined at the level of that entity, as well as from the modules that are defined at the level of the entity's fields. A function that is defined in a component-level Script container can be called from anywhere in the component. In a global ProcScript module, you can create user-defined functions without a function statement. In this case, the function is given the name of the global ProcScript. Operations cannot have a return type and cannot be used as functions. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
